_schema.description: Compat status for upstream Rust crates targeted at wasm32-wasip2. Consulted by tooling/scaffold.py when wiring --crate args.

status_values.clean: builds + runs clean on wasm32-wasip2 with default features
status_values.needs-bootstrap: builds with RUSTC_BOOTSTRAP=1 (nightly-feature gate in tree)
status_values.needs-feature-tweak: builds clean only with non-default feature flags noted in `notes`
status_values.broken: panics or fails to instantiate on wasi-p2; do not use without forking
status_values.hand-roll-preferred: ecosystem doesn't have a usable crate; the inline implementation is short enough that hand-rolling beats fighting any crate (see `notes` for the rough LOC + algorithm hint)
status_values.unverified: not yet evaluated in this catalog

infer.notes: Use default-features = false; lean MIME sniffer with compiled magic table

base62.notes: API takes u128 only, not bytes  doesn't fit BLOB-in/TEXT-out scalar shape. If a caller needs base62, expose as INTEGER-in/TEXT-out scalar pair instead.

url.notes: default-features = false works; idna transitively brought in

emojis.notes: Full Unicode 16 table compiled in (~600 KB component contribution)

sqlparser.notes: DO NOT use default-features = false  drops recursive-protection which is required for the grammar to compile. Component is ~6 MB.

minijinja.notes: Need features = ['builtins','macros','serde']  default-features = false drops serde support and triggers a deprecation warning

phonenumber.notes: ~2.6 MB component  the Google libphonenumber per-country metadata tables are the bulk

lipsum.notes: Requires pinned rand_chacha 0.3 + rand 0.8 for the SeedableRng trait impls to line up

graphql-parser.notes: Use Document<'a, &'a str> for the Text trait param; lifetime parameters need explicit propagation

vader_sentiment.notes: ~2 MB component  the VADER lexicon is the bulk

bt_bencode.notes: Value::Int wraps Number enum (Signed/Unsigned), not raw i64/u64  match Number variants directly

icalendar.notes: REQUIRES features = ['parser'] with default-features = false; otherwise parse() is unavailable

cron.notes: Internally pulls chrono; 5-field crons need normalization to 7-field for cron::Schedule::from_str

chrono.notes: Use features = ['clock'] with default-features = false to keep tz tables out

htmd.notes: Apache-2.0; pulls in html5ever  ~600 KB component contribution. Pair with pulldown_cmark for round-trip.

pulldown-cmark.notes: Use features = ['html'] with default-features = false. NOTE: 0.12 changed Tag::Heading and Tag::Link to STRUCT variants (was tuple in 0.10/0.11); destructure with `{ field, .. }` syntax. Event::End takes TagEnd, not Tag.

similar.notes: For meaningful single-line similarity use from_chars(); from_lines() returns 0.0 on any single-line input pair

tract-onnx.notes: Needs RUSTC_BOOTSTRAP=1 due to typed-path transitive nightly-feature gate. 23 MB component  the full op set (nnef, hir, pulse, transformers) is the bulk. TypedRunnableModel lives in tract_core::prelude (re-exported from tract_onnx::prelude); to_plain_array_view::<f32>() for output reads (not to_array_view, not as_slice).

typed-path.notes: Uses `#![feature(wasip2)]` unconditionally  needs RUSTC_BOOTSTRAP=1 to compile on stable. The feature is a no-op on stable; bootstrap just lets stable rustc accept the gate. Affects any crate that pulls in zip 7.x or calamine.

calamine.notes: Inherits the typed-path bootstrap requirement. ~1 MB component for the full xlsx/xlsb/xls/ods surface.

parquet.notes: Use features = ['arrow','snap'] with default-features = false  pure-Rust compression backends only. flate2 doesn't build for wasi-p2; gzip-compressed Parquet files would need a different feature combination (or upstream patch).

oxigdal-pmtiles.notes: API is `PmTilesReader` (camel-case Pm, not PM)  caught after first attempt used `PMTilesReader::new()`. The `compression` feature pulls oxiarc-archive; skip it if raw-payload-only is acceptable.

hickory-resolver.notes: Use features = ['tokio-runtime','system-config']  pairs with the host's async tokio runtime

zxcvbn.notes: Both versions assume wasm32 = browser context. 3.x uses web_sys::Performance for timing; 2.x uses js_sys::Date::new_0(). Both panic on wasi-p2 with 'cannot call wasm-bindgen imported functions on non-wasm targets' (the irony is the panic site IS wasm). Requires forking or implementing a custom timer wrapper. Abandoned in v1; if needed, hand-roll an entropy estimator (~50 LOC for the common case).

serde_json_path.notes: Already used by web-parsers. RFC 9535 conformant.

eui48.notes: Transitive `rustc-serialize` 0.3.25 fails E0046 on modern rustc (missing trait items on Encodable/Decodable for path types).

_mac-address-parse.notes: ~30 LOC: filter hex digits, parse 12-char string into [u8;6], format with separator. Implementation in extensions/mac/src/lib.rs; copy-edit if you need this elsewhere.

_roman-numerals.notes: ~50 LOC: subtractive pairs lookup table + greedy encode + symbol-pair decode + canonical-form re-encode validation. Range 1..3999; vinculum notation deferred. See extensions/roman/src/lib.rs.

_morse-code.notes: ~150 LOC including the ITU-R M.1677-1 table (letters / digits / punctuation). See extensions/morse/src/lib.rs.

_hex-dump.notes: ~40 LOC formatter. Classic `hexdump -C` shape: offset + hex pairs + ASCII gutter. See extensions/hexdump/src/lib.rs.

mac_address.notes: Cross-platform retrieval of interface MAC  evaluates host network interfaces, not parsing. Not what we want for SQL scalars.

rand_chacha.notes: Pin to 0.3 (not 0.9) when used alongside lipsum 0.9 or other crates expecting the 0.3-era SeedableRng trait shape
